review_text: My parents live in Howell and I looooooove to eat here... but I live an hour away and I have searched and searched for places even 1/2 as good as Los Tres Amigos to no avail.  This place just can't be beat.  Even Mexican Town in Detroit is no match.  The lunch prices at Los Tres are especially good.  The fajittas are to die for.  You get free chips and salsa, food comes out fast and hot, and I've always had good service.  This place is honestly amazing.

Los Tres has a great wait staff and tasty food.  If you want a pitcher of Dos Equis and some great mexican chow with some friends, this is the place.  I've eaten here about a dozen times and the only time service was slow I was with a rather large group.
Food was over priced and very bland...won't be back
I absolutely love the chips and salsa here.  Excellent and fast service.  Go with the enchiladas suiza... you will not be disappointed!

I'm not a huge fan of authentic Mexican cuisine but my wife is so we sometimes venture out to this place. This is as authentic as you are going to get in Howell, I know this because dishes that come with olives are not black nor green but some sort of tan color. I assume they are olives native to Mexico and or the surrounding area. So the food is good if you are into Mexican food. The waitstaff is great. They're very polite and at least on our visits have never screwed anything up. For somebody who doesn't like Mexican food all that much is a tolerable and decent place to go, for somebody who goes ape crap over Mexican food, well they'll probably love it.

review_text: We will not be returning to our once favorite local Mexican restaurant.  We noticed a whole sea of new faces last night as we were seated.  We were handed the new menu and as we opened we not only noticed that it had shrunk drastically but the prices were also drastically higher.  You now have to pay extra for your side of beans or rice whereas before it was included with your meal.  We didn't let that stop is because like I said, it's our go to for mexican.  We ordered some of tjat lovely white cheese dip and when it arrived it was half the size of what you used to get.  It was in a tiny salsa bowl and cost $4.  After 10 chips it was gone.  Again, we still didn't pay that any mind and waited for our order.  By this time kiwe had a familiar face helping our waiter, who seemed to be extremely new and didn't know how to use his voice.  We received our order in a timely manner.  This is where they lost me as a customer.  I ordered fish tacos.  A very easy dish and normally a very good one.  I took one bite and immediately spit it out.  It tasted like they just pulled the fish out of the local creek.  Other than the nasty fish taste it had absolutely no other flavor.  Our waiter ( the one helping the mute boy) promptly removed the fish tacos from our bill and asked if I'd like to order something else.  At that point it was a no from me.  We got our bill and what would normally be a $30 meal for 2 was a $30 bill for 1 even after my meal was taken off the bill.  I went to the front to pay and asked why things have changed so much and was told it was a new manager.  I told her I was sad to say that I wouldn't be returning only to be met with a rude smile and nothing after that.  I'm so disappointed in everything we encountered last night except for our one familiar face, the waiter who tried to salvage my meal.
owner_response: Hello Kim, we are really sorry to hear about your experience. Our menu changed, but our meals (most of them) include rice and beans. Our combo plates are now open to the customers decision, but it comes also with rice and beans. Unfortunately your food was not the best and we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience. We will talk to the manager because we want to offer the best food and service. We hope you will give us another chance.

review_text: My wife and I have been to the Howell location at least 2 dozen times over the last five years. It was always great. However the last 3 times have been the worst ever. I will not go back. Tonight my dinner was brought out 10-15 before my wife’s was. According to our waiter someone else took my wife’s dinner and now we have to wait until they make another one. Nothing like eating while my wife waits for hers.  I asked the waiter for jalapeños with my dinner they never came. When my wife finally got her dinner (fajita taco salad with chicken) it had one piece of chicken. No chance to talk to the manager. No offer for anything else. All the waiter said at least 5 times was that the kitchen messed up. I will not go back ever again. Instead I will just go to Mexican Fiesta in Hartland. Better food and better service.

review_text: We finally went there for the first time last night.  We were pleased it was not too busy at 7:30, our group of 5 got seated immediately.  Unfortunately the manager (maybe owner) who seated us did not even smile, her attitude was as if we were an inconvenience.  The chairs at our table had food debris on them and one was so wobbly we had to switch it for a new seat.  The table itself you could tell had been wiped down, but was sticky and there was salt and food debris in the crack where the two tables had been pushed together.  Gross! Our waitress came right away and was pleasant.  When she brought our drinks, instead of going around the table and serving them, she made us pass them around.  FOOD was AMAZINGLY GOOD!!  Burrito Cochinita phenominal!  Waitress was attentive.  When she came to take empty plates and offer carry out boxes, she dropped off the bill.  Never asked if we wanted dessert....her loss, we would have, instead we stopped somewhere else on the way home for dessert.  As we left, we passed many employees (host, manager, owner?) Nobody said thanks or bye.  Not a friendly atmosphere at all.  My husband and I have worked in food and beverage and service for many years, this is not acceptable service.  I hate to say we will not dine there again.  I might order carry out, but am concerned with the cleanliness of the kitchen based on how dirty the table was.  The burrito Cochinita was so good I might risk it.  Too bad the front of the house was not as good as kitchen....

review_text:
First time in this location based on positive food reviews but unfortunately we did not get a chance to try the food.  I understand businesses, especially restaurants, are short staffed. Your note on the door stating such is a good reminder, however that is not an excuse for a rude server. We entered at noon and was surprised there was only one customer. Yet it took 10 mins for the server to drop of chips which he did and left. We had to wave him over and ask for menus, which took another 3-5 minutes. The server seemed annoyed and slammed down our menus not saying a word and walked away.
Literally another 10 minutes go by and we had to once again wave him over to say we are ready to order. He appeared annoyed not saying a word.  At this point, it was 25 mins after we arrived and our only interaction with our server was when we flagged him down. Then we noticed 2 other tables who sat down way after us had already received their food (but they had a different server). 15 more minutes go by. Still no food and no sign of our server. We decided to leave. I told the gentleman behind the bar about our experience and that we were walking out because of our rude server, waiting 40 minutes and still no food. His response was “okay”. This was the first time ever leaving a restaurant before receiving the meal. Again I understand being short staffed but that should not have impacted our service with initially only 1 other customer there. The rudeness is inexcusable. Overall, horrible experience.
owner_response:
We are very sorry to hear about your experience with us.
We will be speaking with management to prevent this from happening in the future.

review_text: Where do I begin? First and foremost I understand it is “Cinco De Mayo”, but they should have staffed properly. I’m not a hard customer to please, but I at least expect a glass of water to be refilled at least once without having to ask. That’s not even the bad part. Aside from getting my food served cold,  I then proceeded to watch a child projectile vomit all over the floor, 10 feet from me, and by the entrance area where guests were dining and waiting to be seated. Nobody did a thing for almost 10 minutes. You do understand there are proper procedures you’re supposed to follow when dealing with a biohazard such as vomit, especially in a restaurant, right? You guys failed every step of the procedure. You need to do more than slap a mop on it. I was absolutely shocked and more than anything disgusted. More importantly not one person, not even the supposed manager alerted any diners or guests walking in. No apologies, no heads up, nothing. They just kept walking around it until someone eventually came with a mop to slosh it all around. I was sick to my stomach. I will never come back to this establishment and I advise anyone else to avoid dining here if you guys cannot follow simple health procedures. Please do better.

review_text:
We have ordered delivery (or takeout in one case) a few times from this location. The food is actually quite good and we like the menu variety. There is something all 5 of us like. HOWEVER. There are some major problems with ordering online, either through their website or the DoorDash app. Through their website, you cannot order drinks except for a handful of sodas with a kids meal only. And the bigger problem, that is now deterring me from ordering again, is that through either platform, there is NO way to specify NO CILANTRO. Both my husband and I have the genetic mutation that makes cilantro taste like bitter soap. It makes the whole dish taste extremely unpleasant. And Los Tres Amigos PILES on the cilantro, and it's on everything, especially the dish I most want to order- quesabirria! I tried to pick it all off once and it was impossible. Not only is there no box to check for no cilantro, they also don't allow special instructions so there's no way to add a note, either.

I ordered pickup one time through their website and when I spoke to them about my frustration, their solution was to call to order. For one, I shouldn't have to call just to make a basic request- we're ordering for 5 people so it's not a quick order, and ordering verbally over the phone when it's more than 1 or 2 things just creates more opportunities for mistakes. I also get anxiety about making phone calls, and if I have to make a phone call for something non-essential that I SHOULD be able to do online like everyone else, I'm probably just going to order from somewhere else. I use up my quota of pushing through the anxiety to make calls on calls that are actually important.

Some might say just go dine in store. We have 5 people in our family and deal with major medical conditions and have service dogs. It's a huge production to take our family places, and if I'm ordering from a restaurant, it's probably because I'm an exhausted, overworked mom with brain fog and just need to get dinner on the table for my family. I don't have the energy to drag everyone out to a physical restaurant just because a store doesn't have a checkbox for NO CILANTRO when ordering online.

It's 2026, Los Tres Amigos! I know cilantro is a staple of Mexican food, but thanks to science most people know by now that many people genetically cannot stand the taste of cilantro. You don't want people to eat your food and say it tastes like poison or having their mouth washed out with soap! Thanks to the wonders of technology, the solution to get my business back is extremely simple. All you have to do is add a checkbox for NO CILANTRO to ALL menu options that normally contain it (I know it's possible, because there are a couple of taco items that already have this checkbox) on DoorDash and your website. We already have to pay exorbitant prices to order DoorDash/delivery, this doesn't seem like a big ask.

And please don't just dismiss customer concerns about something like this by telling us to add to our stress and order by phone. I'm tired. I'm supporting my husband through terminal brain cancer treatment while raising our 3 teens/tween who are all neurodivergent (just like their parents). I just want to eat a warm quesabirria with NO CILANTRO.
owner_response: Thank you for your detailed feedback, Sharon. We apologize for your less than perfect experience with our cilantro inclusion and online ordering process. We understand how an ingredient like cilantro can greatly impact your overall dining experience. Your suggestions regarding a "No Cilantro" checkbox have been noted, as we're always looking for ways to enhance our service to cater to our customers' needs. We value your feedback and deeply sympathize with the challenges your family is facing. We will certainly consider your proposal in order to make your next experience with us better.
